title: davallia polymorpha
intro: Davallia polymorpha, also known as the Rabbit's Foot Fern, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia and Australia, characterized by its distinctive, divided fronds resembling rabbit's foot. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Davallia polymorpha include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Davallia polymorpha are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing the plant with other herbs, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically as a paste to alleviate symptoms.
